p.strong: The 56-byte boundary (Vector 3) is the single most important test case:

ul.li: 55-byte message → used=56 ≤ 56 → 1 block, 0 zero-fill bytes between marker and length field
56-byte message → used=57 > 56 → 2 blocks, Merkle-Damgård chain required
One extra input byte triggers an entirely different code path and an additional compression call

g.text: ⑥ sha256_compress(H, buf) once; Step 5: serialize H
H not initialized → wrong all hashes
total_bits not zeroed → wrong length field
M3 sigma constant swap → T1/T2 wrong
M3 H[i]= vs H[i]+= → feed-forward lost
⑤ memset(buf, 0, 64); write L=448=0x01C0 at buf[56..63]
⑥ compress BLOCK 1 → chain: H_block1 = f(H_block0, buf)
⑦ Merkle-Damgård: block 1 starts from block 0 output state
